title: Sorting Array of Integers with Incorrect Swap

statement.markdown: Implement a function to sort a given array of integers using the bubble sort algorithm. The function should sort the array in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a list of integers sorted in ascending order.
statement.notes: Note that the array is 0-indexed and the size of the array is given by n. No extra space should be used.

examples.explanation: Sorts the array in ascending order using bubble sort.

hints.text: The bubble sort algorithm works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.

editorial.title: Implementation of Bubble Sort with Swap Fix
editorial.intuition: The bubble sort algorithm works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: We can solve this problem using the brute force approach, where we compare each element with its adjacent element and swap them if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.betterSolution: The bubble sort algorithm can be optimized by breaking the loop as soon as the array is sorted.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses the bubble sort algorithm with a fix to handle the swap operation correctly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness of the bubble sort algorithm is based on the fact that it works by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.
editorial.dryRun: Here is an example run of the bubble sort algorithm: [1, 3, 2, 4] -> [1, 2, 3, 4].
commonMistakes: Not handling the edge case where the array is already sorted
